preamble: The lenition of /d/ to /j/ is a common process in Romance languages, often occurring intervocalically as the voiced dental stop becomes more sonorous and reduces to a semivowel.

examples.note: The Latin intervocalic /d/ in 'hodie' weakened to a palatal glide stage before disappearing or merging into the diphthong in Spanish 'hoy'.
Development of Latin /dj/ to palatal sounds in Italian often passed through a glide or affricate stage.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
